commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
email: add DynamoDB dedup guard to prevent duplicate daily broadcasts

Gunicorn calls create_app() in both master and worker processes, causing
multiple scheduler threads to fire at UTC 00:00 and send duplicate emails.
The --workers 1 fix in CloudFormation only applies to new EC2 instances and
doesn't update the live systemd service file.

Added an atomic DynamoDB conditional put at the start of _do_auto_broadcast()
using the existing ystocker-daily-summaries table. The first process to write
the "broadcast_sent" sentinel for today proceeds; all others exit immediately.
The sentinel expires after 7 days via DynamoDB TTL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Upgrade StockSense to Stock Ranking Framework v4.0

- Updated STOCKSENSE_RULES_CONTEXT with all 10 v4 rules (R1–R10)
- R1 weight 25% → 20%, updated PE band tables with sector band shift
- R2 replaced: Market Cap → Relative Strength vs Nifty50 (rs_12m ratio)
- R3 updated to v4 first-match P/B logic
- R4/R5 updated to compare avg YoY vs sector average
- R6 updated with top-down first-match + R6/R10 divergence flag
- R7 updated TR% thresholds + OBV bearish divergence penalty
- R8 rebuilt as Technical Composite (TREND×0.45 + RSI×0.35 + MACD×0.20) with quality overlay
- R9 more specific 30d event signals
- R10 new: MF Net Buying (NetBuy% size-normalised)
- Updated JSON schema: marketCap→relativeStrength, rsi→technicalComposite, added mfNetBuying
- Default model switched to claude-sonnet-4-6
- Frontend SCORE_FACTORS updated to match v4 keys and weights (10 factors)

v1.8.0: Auto Fish with human-like fidgeting + vanish detection + challenge stats

Auto Fish (F key):
- Detects fish bites via bobber velocity spike, reels in with human reaction time
- Uses proper use-key simulation (press/hold/release) for full rod animation
- Configurable reel delay (150-600ms) and cast delay (400-1200ms)
- Fidgeting: randomly sneaks, strafes left/right while waiting for bites
- Mouse drift: subtle yaw/pitch movement like a hand resting on mouse
- All humanization features individually toggleable
- Full failsafe support: name mention stop, teleport detection, screen-open stop

Also includes:
- Vanish detection (PlayerListMixin): detects staff going invisible
- Challenge stats tracking with adaptive delay targeting
- Entity glow improvements

commit.message:
v0.7.0 — audit enhancements, brew-upgrade, apply-defaults

security-audit.sh:
  - Bluetooth state check via system_profiler (finding: Medium)
  - Developer mode check via DevToolsSecurity (finding: Medium)
  - World-readable files scan: ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.gnupg, ~/.config/op (finding: High)
  - Sudoers NOPASSWD detection (finding: High)
  - New sections: Sensitive File Permissions, Sudoers Configuration

brew-upgrade.sh (mac-deploy-upgrade):
  - brew update → captures outdated list → brew upgrade → brew cleanup
  - Logs dated upgrade summary to private/machines/<hostname>/brew-upgrades.log
  - --dry-run shows outdated packages without installing
  - --no-casks skips cask upgrades

apply-defaults.sh (mac-deploy-defaults):
  - Screen lock: require password immediately, 5-minute screensaver
  - Screenshots: move to ~/Documents/Screenshots, disable shadows
  - Finder: show extensions, hidden files, full path in title bar
  - AirDrop: contacts only
  - Mail: disable remote image loading (tracking pixel protection)
  - Safari: no auto-open downloads, fraud warnings, full URL bar
  - Software Update: auto-check, auto-download, security data (sudo)
  - --dry-run prints each defaults write command without applying

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

YouTube video summarizer CLI that generates structured markdown + HTML
with captioned images. Features:

- 5-stage pipeline: subtitle extraction → Claude API summary →
  frame capture → Vision image selection → markdown/HTML rendering
- STT fallback via mlx-whisper for videos without subtitles
- Stage-level caching for resume and API cost savings
- Local video file support
- Config via ~/.config/yts/config.toml
- Shell wrapper script for easy invocation

commit.message:
reduce slippage 3% -> 0.5% for stablecoin MEV protection

3% slippage on USDC-to-USDC bridges is excessive — allows sandwich
attackers to extract up to $3 per $100 bridged. 0.5% is appropriate
for stablecoin-to-stablecoin swaps with tight spreads.

Also: slippage now configurable via SLIPPAGE env var, and BRAIN_MODEL
env var added to .env.template.

commit.message:
tighten search engine: lower APY cap to 50%, expand quote range to top 10

- yield_scanner: max_apy default 100% -> 50%. Stablecoin yields above 50%
  are almost always unsustainable incentive programs or scams. Aave/Morpho/
  Compound typically max out at 5-30%.
- marco: fetch bridge quotes for top 10 candidates (was top 5). Previously
  a great opportunity ranked #6-10 could never be evaluated because it
  lacked bridge cost data for the brain.
- tests: 58 -> 59 (test default APY cap rejects 80% yield)

Redesign: clean header, card layout, readable typography

Header
  - Remove NavigationStack title bar entirely (.toolbar .hidden)
  - Custom fixed VStack header: tram icon badge + title + count
  - No gradient text, no emoji in title — clean system colors

Month section header
  - Format changed: "yyyy년 M월" → "N월  yyyy" (e.g. "3월  2026")
  - Grouping key: "yyyy-MM" for correct alphabetic sort (Oct/Nov/Dec)
  - Font: bold month + light year, no gradient, no decoration

Card (KTXEventRow)
  - Replaced horizontal 5-element HStack with 3-row VStack layout
    ① Station names (26pt bold) — dep left, arr right with arrow center
    ② Times (18pt monospaced, ktxBlue) + gradient fade line
    ③ Meta bar: lamp · status · train · date · delete button
  - All decorative elements removed; typography drives hierarchy
  - Lamp: orange replaces yellow (better contrast on white)
  - Delete button: subtle circle × (less aggressive)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix stale APY bug, brain error handling, configurable model

Critical bug: brain was making decisions based on pool APY captured at
migration time (could be days stale). Now refreshes current pool APY from
live DefiLlama data each cycle by matching on pool_id or symbol+project+chain.

Also:
- brain: graceful degradation on API failure (returns hold with confidence 0
  instead of crashing the entire cycle)
- brain: model configurable via BRAIN_MODEL env var
- marco: atomic journal writes (tmp+rename pattern)
- tests: 56 -> 58 (brain error handling, no-JSON-output default)
- requirements: add pytest-asyncio

commit.message:
fix: prune orphan branches absorbed into main via rebase merge

Orphan branch pruning only checked for exact SHA match in merged PRs.
Rebase merges create new commit SHAs, so orphan branches from rebased
PRs were never cleaned up. Added the same branch_absorbed_into_main?
fallback already used by stale branch handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix double-save race, async web3 calls, atomic journal, remove dead code

- marco.py: deduct bridge cost BEFORE record_migration() so a single
  save_state() captures both the new chain and the reduced position_usd.
  Previously a crash between two saves would leave position_usd stale.
- marco.py: journal writes now use atomic tmp+rename (matching wallet
  pattern) to prevent corruption on crash.
- lifi.py: wrap ALL sync web3 calls in asyncio.to_thread via _call()
  helper — was blocking the event loop for 0.7-3.5s across 7 calls
  (allowance, get_transaction_count, get_block, eth.call, send_raw_tx).
- lifi.py: remove unused get_routes() function (dead code since inception)
